name: Ajiaco
recipeIngredient: 4 Ears yellow corn, shucked and sliced into 1-inch thick wheels
3 pounds Chicken, cut into bite-sized pieces
2 large Yucca, peeled and cut into inch-thick dice
recipeInstructions:
This recipe is for a Cuban stew/soup that I used to enjoy con mucho gusto. This version of Ajiaco was prepared by Senora Garcia in her little store-front restaurant on Collins Ave. in Miami Beach. Although this is not her exact recipe, it's the closest I could get to it.
1. Melt the butter in a 6-quart pot over medium heat.
2. Cook chicken pieces in the butter until no longer pink. Remove chicken with a slotted spoon and place in a bowl.
3. In the same pot, add onion, garlic, cayenne pepper, and paprika. Cook while stirring until the onion is translucent and colored with the paprika.
4. Add stock, milk, yucca, and corn to the pot. Bring almost to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for about 1 hour, or until yucca is tender. Stir occasionally.
5. Remove from heat and stir in lime juice.
6. Serve with Cuban or French bread that has been sliced and broiled until golden.
As an alternative, frozen corn on the cob can be used, as well as fresh frozen kernels. The fresh corn cob seems to impart a nice flavor to this dish. If you're wondering what to do with the wheels of corn, just pick them up with your fingers and nibble around the rims. This dish received rave reviews at the 1991 Blind Pass Marina Xmas Party.
